title: General Clothing Provision * Homeless Youth - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > General Clothing Provision * Homeless Youth 13 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV At Risk Youth Homeless Families Homeless Men Homeless Youth Homelessness Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ARTISTS MENTORSHIP PROGRAM AMP DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to music lessons, art workshops, recording studio sessions, roleplaying game sessions, cultural outings, meals, hygiene supplies, clothing, first aid supplies, and mentorship services. 16 NW Broadway Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Homeless youth ages 15-25 Hours: Monday-Thursday 4pm-8pm, Friday by appointment only Email (503) 481-4970 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEARTS WITH A MISSION HEARTS WITH A MISSION MEDFORD YOUTH SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Youth shelter providing temporary emergency shelter for up to 120 days with written parent/guardian consent or 72 hours without written consent. Services for sheltered and unsheltered youth include case management, mentoring, housing search assistance, and counseling services. Bathing facilities, laundry facilities, a clothing closet, and personal grooming supplies are also provided. School supplies are for enrolled participants only. 517 Edwards Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Homeless or at-risk youth ages 10-17 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 646-7385 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS ACCESS CENTER ACCESS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Access Center is a service for homeless and runaway youth who are seeking intake assessment and referral. Provides basic needs including food, clothing, basic medical supplies, and shelter. Youth receive an assessment to determine if they meet homeless youth services requirements. For youth in crisis, emergency shelter is provided at Porch Light Youth Shelter. 1635 SW Alder Street Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 18-24 who are homeless or at immediate risk of becoming homeless; eligibility is determined through an assessment with the agency Hours: 7 days per week 9am-5pm Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES ADMIN OFFICE RESOURCE PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelf-stable food pantry, basic personal care items (such as toothbrushes and deodorant), and clothing. 1790 W 11th Avenue Suite 200 Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Homeless and at-risk youth ages 16-24 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 686-2688 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES RURAL PROGRAM LOOKING GLASS RURAL PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a drop-in center with access to food, clothing, and other vital resources. Provides case management services to assist youth in finding and maintaining stable housing. Also helps with employment including resume preparation, job skills training, and connections to employment opportunities. 620 E Whiteaker Avenue Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Youth ages 11-21 who are homeless, runaways, or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 767-3823 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES SPRINGFIELD RESOURCE PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelf-stable food pantry, basic personal care items (such as toothbrushes and deodorant), and clothing. 1403 F Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Homeless and at-risk youth ages 16-24 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 686-2688 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES WEST EUGENE RESOURCE PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelf-stable food pantry, basic personal care items (such as toothbrushes and deodorant), and clothing. 260 E 11th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Homeless and at-risk youth ages 16-24 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 484-4428 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 223 SE M Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 841-1974 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 500 Monroe Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 608-6868 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH DOWNTOWN DROP-IN DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community meals six days per week. Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Provides information about community resources such as education, job training, employment, housing, and LGBTQIA2S+ supports. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. 820 SW Oak Street Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-10am, 1pm-3pm, and 4pm-6:30pm, Saturday 1pm-3pm and 4pm-6:30pm. Meals served for the first hour of each shift. Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH YOUTH CONNECT DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community meals six days per week. Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Provides information about community resources such as education, job training, employment, housing, and LGBTQIA2S+ supports. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. 43 SE 102 Avenue Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Ages 12-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-10am, 1pm-3pm, and 4pm-6:30pm, Saturday 1pm-3pm and 4pm-6:30pm. Meals served for the first hour of each shift. Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH YOUTH OPPORTUNITY CENTER DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. Also connects youth to other services like education, job training, employment, housing, LGBTQIA2S+ supports, and more. 470 SE 165th Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-6pm Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details P:EAR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center that offers arts and music, mentoring, educational, and outdoor recreational programs. Additional supportive services include bus tickets, haircuts, clothing, and support for pregnancy and parenting. Also offers meals and cooking classes. Accepts donations of food, clothing, and camping gear. 338 NW 6th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 15-24 experiencing homelessness Hours: Tuesday-Friday: 9am-2pm Breakfast at 9am and lunch at noon to those served by p:ear Donations: Monday-Friday 8am-3pm Email (503) 228-6677 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARTISTS MENTORSHIP PROGRAM AMP DROP-IN CENTER HEARTS WITH A MISSION YOUTH SHELTER JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS ACCESS CENTER LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES RESOURCE PANTRY LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES LOOKING GLASS RURAL PROGRAM LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES RESOURCE PANTRY LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES RESOURCE PANTRY MASLOW PROJECT GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH DROP-IN DAY SERVICES NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH DROP-IN DAY SERVICES NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH DROP-IN DAY SERVICES P:EAR P:EAR
